sections.paragraph:
προπορεύομαι fut. προπορεύσομαι (X. et al.; POxy 1144, 3; 5; 9 [I/II a.d.]; LXX) go on before w. gen. of pers. (Aristot. 844b, 5; LXX) Ac 7:40 (Ex 32:1, 23). ἐνώπιόν τινος Lk 1:76 shows the influence of OT usage, though the v.l. πρὸ προσώπου τινός is also found in the LXX.—The metaph. προπορεύσεται ἔμπροσθέν σου ἡ δικαιοσύνη B 3:4 (Is 58:8) also has an OT background (but cp. X., Cyr. 4, 2, 23 πορεύεσθε ἔμπροσθεν).—DELG s.v. πόρος. M-M.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

gen. gen. = genitive

pers. pers. = person(s)

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

OT OT = Old Testament

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

metaph. metaph. = metaphor(ically)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 873
